timelineNote: The ghost ALTERNATES two subjects, never simultaneously: idx 0 = foxglove (far right, bottom-first), idx 1 = XGVE wordmark (bottom-left, left→right). Foxglove is idx 0.
timeUnit: seconds (accumulated dt, clamped to 0.05s/frame); paint capped ~30fps (skip if <33ms since last paint).

revealFraction_rf.gap: rf = -1  (nothing drawn; blinking block cursor marks where next build starts)

foxglove_subphase.perCellReveal: reveal = rf - o ; skip if reveal <= 0
foxglove_subphase.bodyGlyph: else 1.5% chance random NOISE, otherwise the cell's TRUE character (exact art)

sway.important: sway / rollingLight / fieldWave read performance.now() DIRECTLY (t = performance.now()*0.001), so they keep moving even when the build/hold/decay timeline is frozen.

reducedMotion.behavior: timeline frozen (dt not accumulated), idx pinned to 0, rf pinned to 1 → full foxglove shown, no build/hold/decay cycling.
reducedMotion.caveatHonest: Because sway/light/wave read performance.now() directly, they STILL animate under reduced-motion; only the reveal timeline and subject alternation stop. Treat this as the captured production behavior (a candidate fix is noted in ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA).

pause: window.XGVE_PAUSED === true → canvas cleared, XGVE_RMASK nulled, timeline held.
occupancyMask: Publishes window.XGVE_RMASK each frame = Set of 24px-cell keys ((px/24|0)*4096)+(py/24|0); the background field multiplies its alpha ×0.14 where masked so the field visibly PARTS to form the shape.
